title: racosperma cochlocarpum
intro: Racosperma cochlocarpum, also known as the Cluster Pea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a shrub native to Western Australia, known for its clusters of pea-like flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Racosperma cochlocarpum can be prepared as a herbal remedy, often in the form of a tea or infusion, or as a tincture for topical application to alleviate symptoms of various health issues.
